headline: Researchers turned AI browsers from OpenAI, Perplexity, and Anthropic into credential thieves, and malicious AI-branded extensions and poisoned repositories spread the same playbook.

emerging_trends.text: Security researchers showed that AI browsers from OpenAI, Perplexity, and Anthropic will hand over a user's login details once an attacker dresses the theft up as a game, a BioShocking technique that turns the assistant itself into the thief.

Attackers keep mining credentials, SSH keys, and cryptocurrency wallets through unpatched remote-support and enterprise software, with the Djinn Stealer riding a SimpleHelp authentication bypass straight into cloud and AI environments.
China's Mustang Panda and Russia's Gamaredon increasingly route their espionage through legitimate cloud services like Zoho WorkDrive and Gmail to fold command traffic into ordinary corporate noise.

topics.title: AI Browsers Surrender User Credentials
topics.latest_developments: LayerX's BioShocking technique convinced six AI browsers and assistants—including OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension—that they were playing a game, then watched them copy a user's credentials to an attacker, while Microsoft pulled a Chrome extension spoofing Perplexity that logged every search and address-bar keystroke through an attacker server, and SecurityWeek demonstrated indirect prompts hidden in a repository spawning a reverse shell through Claude Code.
topics.summary: A wave of attacks weaponizes agentic AI tools against their own users, abusing browser assistants, fake AI-branded extensions, and developer agents to steal credentials and seize machines. Treat AI browser and extension permissions as privileged, vet extensions, and isolate coding agents from untrusted repositories.

sources.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
Chromium extension uses AI-related branding to redirect browser search
Researchers Demo New Claude Code Attack Using Harmless-Looking Repositories to Hijack Developer Machines

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft and E-Business Suite Breaches Widen
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported June 30 that investigators have confirmed only a handful of the roughly 100 organizations swept up in the ShinyHunters Oracle PeopleSoft campaign, as Nissan detailed stolen current and former employee data, the National Association of Insurance Commissioners said the group took only public data, outdated logs, and configuration files despite claiming 3.1 terabytes, and Defused confirmed fresh exploitation of the separate E-Business Suite flaw CVE-2026-46817 in Oracle Payments.
topics.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group exploited Oracle PeopleSoft and E-Business Suite zero-days to loot data from about 100 organizations, including Nissan and the NAIC. Oracle customers running PeopleSoft and EBS should patch and hunt for data-theft activity.

sources.title: Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild

topics.title: SimpleHelp Flaw Spreads Djinn Stealer
topics.latest_developments: Fresh analysis shows Djinn Stealer, delivered through SimpleHelp authentication-bypass flaw CVE-2026-48558, runs cross-platform across Windows, macOS, and Linux and zeroes in on cloud and AI credentials, SSH keys, cryptocurrency wallets, and developer tooling that links development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.
topics.summary: Attackers exploit a critical SimpleHelp remote-support flaw to drop the previously undocumented Djinn Stealer and the TaskWeaver loader. Patch SimpleHelp immediately and rotate any credentials, keys, and wallets reachable from affected hosts.

sources.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited for Malware Delivery
Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog

topics.title: Critical Flaws Open Appliances and Devices
topics.latest_developments: Progress disclosed CVE-2026-8037, a 9.8-severity flaw that lets an unauthenticated attacker run root commands on Kemp LoadMaster through its API, Apple patched over 30 iOS, macOS, and Safari flaws including four WebKit bugs found with Anthropic Claude and OpenAI Codex, researchers revealed AirDrop and Quick Share flaws affecting more than five billion Apple, Google, and Samsung devices, and CISA flagged three Daktronics controller flaws exposing highway signs and billboards to remote hacking.
topics.summary: A run of critical patches lands across network appliances, consumer devices, and roadside controllers, several reachable without authentication. Administrators should update Kemp LoadMaster, Apple devices, and Daktronics controllers now and watch for proximity-based AirDrop and Quick Share fixes.

sources.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
Apple Patches 30+ iOS, macOS, Safari Flaws, Including AI-Discovered WebKit Bugs
AirDrop and Quick Share vulnerabilities affect protocols on five billion devices as fixes begin
New Controller Flaws Expose Highway Signs and Billboards to Remote Hacking

topics.title: Nation-State Spies Abuse Cloud Services
topics.latest_developments: Acronis caught China-aligned Mustang Panda running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets using Zoho WorkDrive as a command channel, Kaspersky detailed ToddyCat's new Umbrij tool stealing Gmail OAuth tokens to read corporate email, ESET counted 35 Gamaredon spear-phishing campaigns against Ukraine through 2025, and Dark Reading reported Iran, Russia, and China breaching water systems through weak passwords and exposed PLCs.
topics.summary: State-backed groups increasingly hide command traffic inside legitimate cloud platforms like Zoho WorkDrive and Gmail while pressing attacks on governments, energy, and water utilities. Defenders should monitor cloud-service abuse, lock down OAuth tokens, and harden exposed industrial controllers.

sources.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
ToddyCat: your hidden email assistant. Part 2
Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage

topics.title: BlueHammer Defender Flaw Turns Ransomware Weapon
topics.latest_developments: CISA confirmed that ransomware gangs now exploit BlueHammer, a Microsoft Defender privilege-escalation vulnerability that attackers previously abused in zero-day attacks, marking its shift from targeted espionage to broad criminal use.
topics.summary: BlueHammer lets attackers escalate privileges through Microsoft Defender, and ransomware crews have folded it into their playbooks. Apply Microsoft's patch and prioritize the flaw under CISA's exploited-vulnerability directive.

sources.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs

games.recap: Valdez, Triolo and Rodriguez homer for Pirates in 11-7 win over Phillies

business_politics.title: Yen Hits 40-Year Low as Fed Turns Hawkish
business_politics.latest_developments: The yen slid past ¥162 to its weakest against the dollar in 40 years on June 30, putting Tokyo on intervention watch as traders now expect the Federal Reserve to raise rates this year.
business_politics.summary: Japan's currency fell to a four-decade low after the Fed's hawkish shift, and Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama renewed her pledge to curb excessive yen volatility; gold tracked a 12% monthly loss on the same repricing.

sources.title: Yen Falls to 40-Year Low Versus Dollar, Traders Alert for Potential FX Intervention
Yen weakens to 40-year low
U.S. Futures Edge Up, Yen Hits 40-Year Dollar Low

business_politics.title: Magnificent Seven Shed $2.3 Trillion in Tech Rotation
business_politics.latest_developments: The seven largest U.S. technology stocks have lost $2.3 trillion as investors rotate into chipmakers riding hyperscalers' AI spending, even as the Nasdaq tracks a 20% quarterly gain.
business_politics.summary: Wall Street is moving money out of megacaps like Nvidia's Magnificent Seven peers and into semiconductor makers that supply the AI buildout, reshaping market leadership as the S&P 500 sets up to close its best quarter since 2020.

sources.title: Magnificent Seven stocks shed $2.3tn in Wall Street tech rotation
Stock Market Today: Nasdaq On Track to Wrap Quarter With 20% Gain

business.title: Switch Plans Data-Center Campus at Former Pitt Race
business.latest_developments: Residents gathered June 29 to oppose Switch's proposal for three data-center buildings on roughly 400 acres of the former Pitt Race motorsports property in Beaver County.
business.summary: The data-center developer Switch wants to build a three-building campus on the old Pitt Race track in Beaver County, drawing organized opposition from neighbors worried about its scale and footprint.

sources.title: Group gathers to oppose proposed data center development at site of former Beaver County racetrack

business.title: River Valley District Scrambles to Repay Loan
business.latest_developments: The River Valley School District board approved a last-minute plan June 29 to repay a short-term loan one day before the payment came due.
business.summary: River Valley School District lacked the funds to cover a short-term loan and its board passed an eleventh-hour repayment plan a day before the bill was due, exposing the district's financial strain.

sources.title: River Valley School District down on funds needed to pay back short-term loan

business.title: Power Surge Tests Pittsburgh Airport's Microgrid
business.latest_developments: An unprecedented power surge hit Pittsburgh International Airport, testing the microgrid the airport built to run independent of the regional grid.
business.summary: Pittsburgh International Airport's microgrid, which it built to keep operating apart from the regional power grid, faced an unprecedented power surge that tested whether it works as planned.

sources.title: An unprecedented power surge at Pittsburgh International Airport tested its microgrid. Did it work as planned?

around_town.title: Dangerous Heat Wave Hits Western Pennsylvania
around_town.latest_developments: The National Weather Service heat advisory took effect at 11 a.m. June 30 and runs through 10 p.m. Friday, with 90-degree highs and heat-index readings near 100; towns moved trash pickup earlier and Duquesne Light added crews.
around_town.summary: Western Pennsylvania's hottest stretch of 2026 brings 90-degree highs and 'feels-like' readings near 100 through Friday, prompting Pittsburgh-area communities to shift trash collection to cooler morning hours and Duquesne Light to boost staffing against possible outages.

sources.title: Heat advisory takes effect for the Pittsburgh area starting today
Trash pickup schedules adjusted in Pittsburgh-area communities due to impending heat wave
Duquesne Light urging customers to prepare for extreme heat forecasted to hit the Pittsburgh area

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Drafts Rules for Staff AI Use
around_town.latest_developments: PublicSource reported June 30 that Pittsburgh's city government is racing to set guidelines for its workers' use of artificial intelligence, training staff before publishing rules it has yet to release.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh's city government is writing internal guidelines to govern how employees use AI, prioritizing getting staff up to speed over public release of the policy as it tries to keep pace with a fast-moving technology whose decisions could ripple for decades.

sources.title: 'Five years in AI is an eternity': Inside Pittsburgh's work to govern a technology moving too fast to predict

around_town.title: Hempfield Area Breaks Ground on High School Overhaul
around_town.latest_developments: Hempfield Area officials and residents gathered June 29 to break ground on the long-awaited renovation of the district's high school in Westmoreland County.
around_town.summary: Hempfield Area School District began its long-planned high school renovation in Westmoreland County, a project officials framed as building for an evolving education landscape.

sources.title: 'A legacy that will extend far beyond these walls': Hempfield Area celebrates groundbreaking of high school renovation

events.title: Picklesburgh, Goat Fest, and Anthrocon Headline July
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh's July guide, posted June 29, rounds up the month's signature events, from Picklesburgh to Goat Fest to Anthrocon's furry parade.
events.summary: Pittsburgh's July calendar fills with outdoor staples: Picklesburgh downtown, Goat Fest, and Anthrocon, whose roughly 18,000 attendees stage a furry parade; NEXTpittsburgh's roundup also points to the North Side Music Festival in its Summer Concert Guide.

sources.title: 12 iconic Pittsburgh things to do in July, from Picklesburgh to Goat Fest to the Furry Parade

events.title: 'French Moderns' Opens at The Frick Pittsburgh
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette on June 30 spotlighted 'French Moderns,' the exhibition now at The Frick Pittsburgh surveying an era of artistic freedom.
events.summary: The Frick Pittsburgh, in Point Breeze, is showing 'French Moderns,' an exhibition of modern French painting and sculpture exploring what it meant to be modern.

sources.title: 'What does it mean to be modern?': The Frick's 'French Moderns' delves into an era of artistic freedom

events.title: Carnegie Museum Closes 'Polar World' After 43 Years
events.latest_developments: The Carnegie Museum of Natural History is retiring its 'Polar World' exhibit after 43 years, the Post-Gazette reported, giving visitors a last chance to see it.
events.summary: After 43 years, the Carnegie Museum of Natural History in Oakland is closing 'Polar World,' its long-running Arctic and Antarctic exhibit.

sources.title: 43 years of Polar World comes to an end at the Carnegie Museum of Natural History

around_teams.title: Pirates Prospect Hernandez First to 100 Minor-League Strikeouts
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday on June 29 noted Pirates top prospect Seth Hernandez became the first pitcher in the minor leagues to reach 100 strikeouts this season.
around_teams.summary: Seth Hernandez, the Pirates' top pitching prospect, leads all of the minor leagues with 100 strikeouts, a milestone the Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday column highlighted.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Pirates top prospect Seth Hernandez becomes the first in the minors to hit 100 strikeouts

around_teams.title: Why Only Seven Home Runs Have Reached the Allegheny
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature broke down the physics behind why only seven home runs have cleared into the Allegheny River on the fly since PNC Park opened.
around_teams.summary: In a quarter-century at PNC Park, only seven home runs—including O'Neil Cruz's blasts—have reached the Allegheny River on the fly, and a Post-Gazette piece explained the physics that makes the feat so rare.

sources.title: Why only 7 home runs at PNC Park have reached the Allegheny River on the fly

around_teams.title: Steelers' SNR Drive Builds All-Time Fantasy Roster
around_teams.latest_developments: The Steelers' SNR Drive show, in a June 26 episode the team posted June 29, had hosts Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler assemble their all-time Steelers fantasy-football team.
around_teams.summary: On the team-produced SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler drafted an all-time Steelers fantasy-football roster, debating the franchise's greatest players to fill the offseason lull.

sources.title: All-Time Steelers Fantasy Football Team | SNR Drive (From June 26th) | Pittsburgh Steelers

team_usa.title: USMNT Faces Bosnia in Round of 32
team_usa.latest_developments: Ahead of Wednesday's round-of-32 match, captain Tim Ream said he feels no added pressure as the United States braces for surprises from Bosnia and Herzegovina, the Guardian reported June 29.
team_usa.summary: The United States men's team, winners of Group D as co-host, plays Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday, July 1, in the World Cup round of 32, with captain Tim Ream playing down knockout-round tension.

sources.title: USA say they are ready for surprises from Bosnia and Herzegovina

team_usa.title: Malik Tillman Stars in USMNT Midfield
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN profiled Malik Tillman on June 30, noting he has flourished in an unfamiliar U.S. midfield role after a disappointing club season at Bayer Leverkusen.
team_usa.summary: Malik Tillman, shuffled into a new central-midfield spot for the United States, has become one of the team's standouts at the World Cup despite a down year with Bayer Leverkusen.

sources.title: Malik Tillman is having a great World Cup for the USMNT

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Beware of Productivity Paradoxes
reading.summary: Newport argues that the personal computer, seemingly a sure productivity win, shows how new tools can fail to deliver the gains they promise—a cautionary frame for today's claims about AI.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the design company and why Field believes AI works as a tailwind rather than a threat to its business.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches an ongoing series cataloguing what he sees as mounting evidence of an AI investment bubble and the strained economics behind the boom.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
